DISCOVERY 301, DEFENDANTS MALCOLM YEUNG, AND CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC'S' MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION IS GRANTED. PURSUANT TO CCP 2025.450(A), THE COURT ORDERS PLAINTIFF TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION WITHIN 15 COURT DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER UNLESS THE PARTIES MUTUALLY AGREE TO A LATER DATE. PLAINTIFF SHALL MEET AND CONFER WITH DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL WITHIN FIVE COURT DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER TO SET THE DEPOSITION DATE. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS WOULD BE UNJUST AND DOES NOT AWARD THEM. (CCP 2025.450(G)(1).) (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal As A Terminating Sanction; Attorneys' Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Lynn Hollenbeck In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC'S Motion For Dismissal As A Terminating Sanction; Attorneys' Fees And Costs

Defendant Chinatown Community Development Center, Inc. (CCDC) seeks an order dismissing this case in its entirety as a terminating sanction and seeks further monetary sanctions. The court grants in part and denies in part the motion. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING)

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - DEFENDANTS MALCOLM YEUNG, AND CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC's Motion For Court Order To Deem Request For Admission, Set Two Admitted And Request For Sanctions.

Defendants' motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in Chinatown Community Development Center's (CCDC) Requests for Admission, Set Two, is granted. Plaintiff failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set Two, is deemed admitted by Plaintiff. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

Defendants also seek sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $1000 payable by Plaintiff to CCDC. Plaintiff shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MALCOLM YEUNG, AND CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition And Request For Sanctions.

Defendants' motion to compel Plaintiff to appear for deposition is granted. The Hollenbeck declaration submitted with the motion demonstrates that Defendants have repeatedly tried to procure Plaintiff's deposition and have served a notice of deposition, most recently on October 15, 2024. Plaintiff's then-counsel objected to the noticed date but did not provide an alternative date. The court finds Defendants' meet and confer efforts to be reasonable but notes that Plaintiff has been self-represented since January 22, 2025. Pursuant to CCP 2025.450(a), the court orders Plaintiff to appear for deposition within 15 court days of entry of this order unless the parties mutually agree to a later date. Plaintiff shall meet and confer with Defendants' counsel within five court days of entry of this order to set the deposition date.

Defendants also seek sanctions. Because Plaintiff is now self-represented and there is no evidence before the court that Defendants have communicated with Plaintiff to set her deposition since her prior counsel was relieved, the court finds that the imposition of sanctions would be unjust and does not award them. (CCP 2025.450(g)(1).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff?S Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two And Request For Sanctions (Motions)
Off Calendar Mar-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MALCOLM YEUNG, AND CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two And Request For Sanctions.

Defendants' motion to compel Plaintiff to respond to Chinatown Community Development Center's (CCDC) Form Interrogatories, Set Two, is granted. Plaintiff failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2030.290(a).) Plaintiff is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Defendants also seek sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Considering all the circumstances here including that Plaintiff is self-represented, the court does not find substantial justification and awards sanctions of $1000 payable by Plaintiff to CCDC. Plaintiff shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Defendant's unopposed motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories, Set Two, and for sanctions is granted.

Plaintiff failed to respond to the interrogatories timely and has thereby waived objections. Plaintiff is ordered to respond to Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Nos. 69-104, within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. Defendant further seeks sanctions. Order signed.
